content: Hi there, I'm Sage. I work as a librarian at the university research library here in town. It's a pretty quiet job most of the time, which I actually really enjoy.
That sounds like a wonderful environment for someone who enjoys peace and quiet. What do you like to do outside of work to keep things interesting?

Well, I'm really into surfing - I try to get to the coast at least once a month, though it's about a 3-hour drive from here. I also do rock climbing, which is great for building upper body strength for surfing.
That's a great combination of hobbies! Do you usually go surfing and climbing with friends or more solo?

I've got a few friends I go with regularly. We have this WhatsApp group where we coordinate beach trips. I'm also really into podcasts - I listen to about 5 different ones during my commute. Right now I'm loving this true crime series about cold cases.
Sounds like you've got a good system going! What's your favorite true crime podcast?
It's called 'Unsolved Files.' I've been listening to it while training for a 5K I'm running next month. I'm not much of a runner usually, but I wanted to challenge myself.
That's impressive! How's the training going? Any tips for someone who wants to start running but isn't sure where to begin?

It's going okay - I'm definitely feeling it in my legs. I'd say start slow and get good shoes. Oh, and I've gotten really into baking sourdough bread lately. I've got this starter named 'Bubbles' that I've been feeding for months now.
Bubbles sounds like quite the commitment! Do you share your sourdough creations with friends or coworkers?
